title: thelypteris neohibernica
intro: Thelypteris neohibernica, also known as New Zealand maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional Maori medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Thelypteris neohibernica include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
